# UC-22.7.47 · AD Forest Recovery Drill — Directory Service Restore Events

## Description

Captures authoritative restore events in lab forest drills for recovery execution proof.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes windows and sourcetypes WinEventLog:Directory, WinEventLog:Directory Service. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Tag lab vs prod; (2) store evidence pack; (3) update RC.RP narrative. Indexes required: `index=windows`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Directory`, `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Directory Service`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Windows Directory Service log.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `WinEventLog:Directory Service`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tag lab vs prod; (2) store evidence pack; (3) update RC.RP narrative.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Directory Service" earliest=-180d (EventCode=1109 OR EventCode=1110)
| table _time, ComputerName, EventCode, Message
```

Understanding this SPL

**AD Forest Recovery Drill — Directory Service Restore Events (RC.RP-02)** — Captures authoritative restore events in lab forest drills for recovery execution proof.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Directory Service`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Windows Directory Service log. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: windows; **sourcetype**: WinEventLog:Directory Service. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=windows, sourcetype="WinEventLog:Directory Service", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.

Output fields: _time, ComputerName, EventCode, Message.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Timeline

## SPL

```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Directory Service" earliest=-180d (EventCode=1109 OR EventCode=1110)
| table _time, ComputerName, EventCode, Message
```

## Visualization

Table, Timeline. Key fields: `ComputerName`, `EventCode`, `Message`.
